overview: This step aligns paragraphs (TextUnits) from two documents — a source and a target. It internally calls the `ResourceSimplifier` to flatten events and expand TextUnits so all are available for alignment. **Only TextUnit events are passed along — all other event types are discarded.** The step also sends a special `EventType.PIPELINE_PARAMETERS` event to inform subsequent steps (e.g., Sentence Aligner) that the target input has been consumed and is no longer available.

outputOneToOneMatchesOnly.help: Controls whether the step outputs only paragraph pairs where exactly one source paragraph aligns to one target paragraph (1-to-1 matches). When enabled, paragraphs involved in 1-to-many, many-to-1, or many-to-many alignments are discarded from the output. This is useful when downstream steps (e.g., Sentence Aligner) require clean, unambiguous paragraph pairs.
notes: Enabling this discards any paragraphs that could not be cleanly aligned 1-to-1, which may result in content loss in the output.

useSkeletonAlignment.help:
Uses the document skeleton (structural markup around text) as additional anchor points to improve paragraph alignment accuracy. The `ResourceSimplifier` makes skeleton data available for diffing when this option is enabled.

This is marked **experimental** and may improve results for formats where structural markup provides reliable alignment cues.
notes: **Experimental feature** — results may vary across document formats.
Standard Gale and Church alignment (used when this is disabled) is not accurate for long runs of paragraphs. Enabling skeleton alignment may improve results in those cases.

dependsOn.condition: skeleton alignment may produce more 1-1 matches by providing better anchor points, improving the usefulness of the 1-1 filter

limitations: Standard Gale and Church alignment is not accurate for long runs of paragraphs.
All non-TextUnit events (document parts, skeleton, sub-documents, etc.) are lost — only TextUnit events survive the step.
After this step runs, the target input is consumed and unavailable to subsequent steps (signaled via `PIPELINE_PARAMETERS` event).
processingNotes: The `ResourceSimplifier` is called internally to flatten and expand all events before alignment.

A special `EventType.PIPELINE_PARAMETERS` event is sent downstream to signal that the target input has been consumed — steps like the Sentence Aligner use this to know the target document is no longer directly available.
Requires both source and target language to be set in the pipeline.
